best_for: Modern JS/TS stacks wanting the fastest DX: React Email templates, simple API, good free tier
avoid_when: Very high volume where per-email cost dominates; teams needing decades of deliverability tooling
tradeoffs: Best-in-class DX and template story; younger deliverability reputation than the incumbents

best_for: Deliverability-critical transactional email (receipts, password resets) — consistently top inbox-placement reputation
avoid_when: Bulk/marketing sends (they actively police stream separation)
tradeoffs: Elite transactional deliverability and visibility tooling; pricier per email, strict about what you send

best_for: High volume at the lowest unit cost, teams already on AWS with the ops maturity to manage reputation themselves
avoid_when: Wanting managed reputation, dashboards, and support out of the box — SES is a raw pipe
tradeoffs: ~10x cheaper than managed providers at scale; you own warmup, monitoring, suppression, and dashboards

best_for: Established mid-market default when the org already uses them or needs their marketing+transactional combo
avoid_when: Greenfield projects — Resend/Postmark beat them on DX and focus respectively
tradeoffs: Feature-broad and battle-tested; shared-IP neighborhoods and legacy consoles are common complaints

best_for: Newsletters, drip campaigns, audience segmentation, no-code campaign editing for non-developers
avoid_when: Sending marketing through your transactional provider/stream (see EM007)
tradeoffs: Purpose-built segmentation/automation; second vendor, second contact list to keep in sync (webhook/API sync required)

best_for: Almost never — only extreme data-residency mandates with dedicated email-ops expertise
avoid_when: Everyone else — inbox providers distrust unknown IPs by default
tradeoffs: Total control; you inherit the entire deliverability war (IP reputation, blocklists, FBLs) as a full-time job

best_for: Every product sending both: different domains or at least subdomains + separate provider streams, so a marketing complaint spike can't sink password-reset delivery
avoid_when: Nothing — this separation is effectively mandatory practice
tradeoffs: Reputation isolation between must-deliver and nice-to-deliver mail; slightly more DNS/config to maintain

option: React Email / MJML for HTML templates
best_for: Maintainable, testable templates: components + props instead of hand-edited table-soup HTML
avoid_when: A product that only ever sends plain-text (fine — plain text deliverability is great)
tradeoffs: Sane authoring with correct-across-clients output; adds a build step to the email path

best_for: Reply-to-comment, support-ticket creation, email-in workflows via Postmark/SES/Mailgun inbound endpoints
avoid_when: Building your own IMAP poller — webhook-based inbound is strictly better
tradeoffs: Turns email into a webhook like any other integration; parsing real-world MIME still has sharp edges

best_for: Mailpit/Mailtrap or provider sandbox mode locally and in CI — real templates, zero real delivery
avoid_when: Sending real email from non-production — one seeded staging DB away from emailing every customer
tradeoffs: Full-fidelity preview without risk; one env var per environment to configure
